summary: Matched `src/melee/it/items/itluigifireball.c` at 868 bytes by replacing the inline/guarded ASM implementation of `it_802C0368` with C. The key matching detail was extracting the 2D velocity magnitude calculation into a small `static double calc_dist_2d_accurate(Vec3* v)` helper that calls `sqrtf_accurate(VEC2_SQ_LEN(*v))`, allowing the prior unused-stack workaround and full assembly block to be removed. The PR reached 100% for the file and all listed functions, with no review comments recorded.
what_changed: Removed the `ASM`/`MUST_MATCH` guarded assembly implementation of `it_802C0368`.
Added `static double calc_dist_2d_accurate(Vec3* v)` to wrap `sqrtf_accurate(VEC2_SQ_LEN(*v))`.

Converted `it_802C0368(Item_GObj* gobj)` to normal C while preserving calls to `it_8026D9A0`, `it_8027781C`, `GET_ITEM`, `GET_JOBJ`, attribute access through `ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes`, velocity threshold check against `attrs->xC`, and conditional `efAsync_Spawn` effect IDs.
Removed a previous `@todo Unused stack` workaround under `MUST_MATCH`.
Removed a previous `@todo Missing #efAsync_Spawn param?` comment after expressing the effect spawn calls in matching C.
The PR report shows `src/melee/it/items/itluigifireball.c` at 868 bytes, score 0, 100.00%, including `it_802C0368` at 296 bytes and other listed functions at 100%.
smart_moves: Factored the velocity magnitude expression into a separate `static double` helper instead of inlining it directly in `it_802C0368`; this appears to have produced the desired codegen/stack behavior without an artificial unused stack array.
Kept the helper narrowly scoped to the translation unit and named it descriptively around the matching-sensitive behavior: `calc_dist_2d_accurate`.
Used existing math/macros (`sqrtf_accurate`, `VEC2_SQ_LEN`) rather than manually spelling out the reciprocal-square-root sequence from assembly.
Preserved item/effect branch structure in readable C: Luigi fire uses effect ID `1288`, non-Luigi branch uses the alternate effect ID visible in the diff excerpt as `0x4B2`/1202.
Reduced the file by deleting 93 lines of assembly while adding only 7 C lines, leaving a compact matched source.
decomp_lessons: When an inlined math expression causes a mismatch, moving it into a tiny helper can alter evaluation/storage enough to match the target assembly without dummy stack variables.
For item velocity magnitude checks, `sqrtf_accurate(VEC2_SQ_LEN(vec))` is the reusable high-level form corresponding to the long accurate square-root sequence seen in PowerPC assembly.
Avoid retaining `MUST_MATCH` dummy stack hacks once a cleaner C factoring explains the stack/codegen behavior.
A wrapper returning `double` around `sqrtf_accurate` can be matching-relevant; future work should consider return type and helper boundaries when reproducing floating-point codegen.
Use known item access macros (`GET_ITEM`, `GET_JOBJ`) and article-data paths directly when they match the assembly loads cleanly.
naming_conventions: New local helper uses lower_snake_case with a behavior-oriented name: `calc_dist_2d_accurate`.
Existing unmatched item functions remain address-based names such as `it_802C0368`, `it_802C031C`, and `it_802C0490`.
Item constants follow existing enum-style casing such as `It_Kind_Luigi_Fire`.

Struct/member naming in the matched code follows project conventions visible in the diff: `Item_GObj`, `Item`, `HSD_JObj`, `itUnkAttributes`, `xC4_article_data`, `x4_specialAttributes`, `x40_vel`, and `xBC0`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a full `nofralloc` assembly block with C by matching the accurate square-root path through `sqrtf_accurate`.
Used `VEC2_SQ_LEN(*v)` to express squared X/Y velocity length, corresponding to assembly loading `x40_vel` and `x44` and summing their squares.
Factored `sqrtf_accurate(VEC2_SQ_LEN(ip->x40_vel))` into `calc_dist_2d_accurate(&ip->x40_vel)` to avoid the previous manual unused stack allocation.

Kept `efAsync_Spawn(gobj, &(GET_ITEM(gobj)->xBC0), 1, 1288, jobj)` form for the Luigi-fire branch; the diff evidence shows the previous assembly passed `gobj`, `item + 0xBC0`, `1`, effect ID `0x508`, with varargs setup via `crclr 6`.
Deleted labels such as `lbl_802C041C`, `lbl_802C0430`, `lbl_802C045C`, `lbl_802C0478`, and `lbl_802C047C` by recovering structured C control flow.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that introduce tiny static helpers to force matching floating-point codegen.
Find other item files using `sqrtf_accurate(VEC2_SQ_LEN(...))` for velocity or distance thresholds.
Search for remaining `@todo Unused stack` or dummy stack arrays that may be removable through helper extraction.
Search for other `efAsync_Spawn` callsites using item `xBC0` and effect IDs near `0x508` or `0x4B2`.
